architecture_fit.note: Hardcodes attn[j]=0.8 by index; not a transformer head / QK circuit, so it asserts mirror routing rather than showing attention can express it.

baseline_comparison.note: Uses the task-provided uniform baseline with clear lift (~0.83 at L=16) but adds no strawman of its own.

faithfulness.note: No model and no causal/ablation evidence; README does not even propose what such a check would look like.

operating_range.note: Mirror mass stays ~0.89 across the 8–48 sweep (robustness 0.995), but range is <1 order of magnitude and constant by construction.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is genuinely hand-written, but it is a trivial positional index rather than a hand-set QK circuit.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails to parse (positional arg after keyword), np is unimported, and the demo lambda returns zeros/None instead of calling model_fn — viz does not boot.

visualisation_rationale.note: README argues for showing the raw attention vector, a defensible choice, but the described viz is broken and trivial.

notes: The hand-built head hits the headline metrics (mirror mass ~0.89, robustness 0.995, argmax 1.0) by directly writing mass onto the mirror index, but that is an assertion of the answer rather than a demonstration that an attention mechanism expresses positional mirror routing — no QK circuit, no model, and no causal/ablation evidence. The Gradio app does not even parse (syntax error, missing import, demo never calls model_fn), so the visualisation is unusable. Strong automated numbers cannot redeem weak architecture fit and faithfulness, so this fails.
